transport_contract.authentication: HttpAuthMiddleware: a split-space second segment of exactly 36 bytes selects API-key lookup regardless of scheme; otherwise host-only browser session
transport_contract.authorization: create requires owner or active organization membership and an actor-created personal folder; progress/complete require the actor-owned video and disclose foreign or absent targets as 404
transport_contract.body: bounded application/json; the released caller sends no Idempotency-Key and Frame rejects one instead of inventing retry identity

create_contract.identity: every accepted request creates a fresh collision-safe native UUID plus immutable 15-character Cap video alias
create_contract.organization: optional Cap organization alias, otherwise the actor active organization; active owner/member authority is reasserted in the write batch
create_contract.folder: optional Cap folder alias must identify a nondeleted actor-created, null-space folder in that organization
create_contract.title: remove one final extension using /\.[^/.]+$/ then ECMAScript trim; empty becomes Mobile Upload
create_contract.extension: validated final filename extension, otherwise quicktime=mov, webm=webm, matroska=mkv, x-msvideo=avi, x-m4v=m4v, default mp4
create_contract.upload_target: R2 SigV4 PUT capability for the exact key and content type, valid 1800 seconds
create_contract.response_shape: id, shareUrl, rawFileKey, put upload target, and newly-created MobileCapSummary

progress_contract.normalization: Math.max(0, Math.trunc(value)) for both fields, then uploaded=min(uploaded,total)
progress_contract.target_tightening: finite values outside JavaScript's safe-integer range are rejected instead of being rounded by D1 bindings
progress_contract.ordering: the server timestamp fences writes; the latest accepted request wins and equal retries converge
progress_contract.projection: native video_uploads and legacy_mobile_cap_uploads_v1 are updated in the same D1 batch

completion_contract.key: must equal the exact minted rawFileKey; source prefix-only acceptance is intentionally tightened to reject traversal and sibling objects
completion_contract.r2_evidence: HEAD must observe the exact nonempty object and an optional contentLength must equal observed bytes
completion_contract.durability: one immutable provider-pending processing intent per video plus exact authority/postcondition assertions
completion_contract.production_behavior: return 503 provider_execution while the workflow intent is pending; success=true is returned only after independently admitted submission evidence exists
completion_contract.non_fabrication: provider-pending intent does not set the upload phase to processing and does not create a fake media job or workflow receipt

security_tightenings: R2 replaces generic S3/Google Drive writable providers for this deployment
PUT adds If-None-Match: * so the minted object cannot overwrite existing bytes
rawFileKey must equal the server-minted key rather than merely sharing a lexical prefix
completion verifies nonempty R2 bytes and optional length before durable orchestration
all JSON numbers crossing D1 are finite JavaScript-safe integers where byte semantics require integers
